programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star(n)

programme.title: NCIS

Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS

While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently are called in to investigate the death of a miner, which is being treated as suspicious because even though his body was discovered down the pit, records show he was above ground when he disappeared. Alleged victim Arthur Hawkes was a proud union man, and his children confirm he fought long and hard to keep the closure-threatened mine open. But as the detectives dig deeper, they find that not everyone agrees with this portrait of him. Could he really have sold his union comrades down the river? Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Simon Greenall, Jack Deam and Michael Socha(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: With Sasha back on fighting form, UCOS investigates the death of a talented cricket prodigy, who was the apple of his father's eye, yet loathed by his on-field team-mates. The death was recorded as misadventure but with new evidence pointing to infighting, over-zealous autograph hunters and extra marital affairs, UCOS are soon bowled over by the list of potential murder suspects. Meanwhile, wise to Steve's monetary problems, Danny appoints himself as his personal financial adviser. Detective drama, starring Larry Lamb(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jim investigates the murders of a Glasgow by-election candidate and her agent, who had been championing the return of capital punishment. However, the veteran detective finds himself hard-pressed to arrive at a quick solution to the crime. Elsewhere, a young woman is strangled by her stalker then raped. Robert Carlyle makes one of his earliest screen appearances, alongside star Mark McManusrs(n)

programme.desc: As the Brokenwood Golf Club gathers for its Wednesday morning round, a zombie-like figure lurches from the morning mist identified as Alison Stone, the wealthy club owner. Her face red and blistering, she collapses into a bunker and dies leaving Mike and Kristin to investigate an apparent homicide by poisoning. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson(n)

programme.desc: Anna Westin, a troubled childhood friend of Wallander's daughter, visits his house late at night begging for his help - but disappears soon afterwards. Meanwhile, an elderly woman's burned body is found in a shallow grave, and when the detective discovers a connection between Anna and the murder victim, he fears she could be the next target of a dangerous religious fanatic. Linda returns to Ystad to help with the investigation, and has life-changing news for her father. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan (Rome), with Kenneth Branagh and Jeany Spark(n)

programme.desc: The barrister reluctantly agrees to act on behalf of a recently released jailbird accused of kidnapping his infant son, and gets caught up in a bitter tug-of-love battle. John Thaw stars in the legal drama, with guest appearances by Dougray Scott and Toyah Willcox(n)

Police drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman(n)

Jim continues to struggle with his gambling addiction and chilling CCTV footage of Abi makes Samantha fear for her daughter's safety(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kerry Young's funeral casts a sombre shadow over Sun Hill as the team gather to pay their last respects to their colleague, but any hopes of a dignified send-off are dashed by an emotional outburst from Gabriel. Debt-ridden Jim fears an investigation into illegal goings-on at the greyhound track will expose his own complicity, while the close professional relationship between Jack and Debbie turns personal(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Debbie is assigned a major drugs investigation - much to Phil's irritation and Jim's alarm - but the chance to prove herself takes second place to family concerns when she learns her son has sustained internal bleeding from his fall. The bad news prompts her to hand in her resignation, leaving Jack wondering what the future holds for their relationship. Andrea tells Smithy that Gabriel raped Kerry, whose death continues to take its toll on the renegade copper(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The offer of a substantial reward inspires Hetty to find the bright spark behind a spate of arson attacks, and she begins by investigating the activities of a neighbour's foster daughter whose absences from home coincide with the fires. Housewife-detective series, starring Patricia Routledge, with Derek Benfield and Dominic Monaghan(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The detectives find themselves swamped with work after their recent success. Hetty turns her attention to a troubled village, while Robert and Geoffrey embark on a case of their own. Private eye drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Cinder Path
programme.desc: Feature-length drama about one man's journey through love and war in the early part of the 20th century. Charlie MacFell returns from university to the family farm, where his strict father still rules with an iron fist - a situation that soon changes thanks to a misunderstanding with a foolish farmhand. Lloyd Owen stars, with Catherine Zeta-Jones, Tom Bell and Maria Mile(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. They decide the best way to convince him is to have her exhumed - only to be faced with more questions than answers when it turns out the body is not hers after all. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Part one of two. The body of a Falklands War veteran who became one of the leading lights in the campaign against nuclear weapons is found in a stretch of river running underneath the Ministry of Defence. As Boyd and the team set about investigating the man's former allies, they stumble upon footage of an incident intelligence services had tried to cover up - and Grace is reunited with a former lover from her own time in the anti-nuclear movement. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe, Into the Storm), John McArdle (Brookside) and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral, Spooks)(n)

Later, Jules then travels to Leicestershire to meet a husband and wife at their impressive new home and discovers how their move away from a large urban town has helped strengthen family ties(n)

programme.desc: Kush is thrilled to learn Sonia and Martin are back together but when the latter arrives home he finds a letter that leaves him rocked. Honey forces herself to eat in front of the children, making herself feel unwell, while Dotty continues to try to charm Leo - only for him to show his true colours when she mentions his father. Jean and Daniel are the happiest they have ever been, and Rainie sets up a date for Callum(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a 1960s-set horror tale, with Robert as the mysterious Albert Fortenoy welcoming a young family to his crumbling old house. A number of clever theatrical horror devices are used to create a creepy atmosphere and plenty of jump scares - and every single one manages to go horribly wrong. Worse, the designers have taken 'crumbling' to heart and the cast members proceed to fall off, out of and through everything. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: Gary's under-9s little league team, The Badgers, are in the cup final. However, the week before the big match his star player, Abboussi, is poached by Chelsea. Gary throws absolutely everything at getting him back and even turns up, unannounced, at Abboussi's father's workplace to try to convince him that his son is making a big mistake. Big Gary bets against his own son's side to earn a few quid, and Terri is stumped to learn mother-in-law, Denise, is new best buddies with Chloe Ferdinando, Comedy, starring Tom Davis and Laura Checkley, with Simon Day, Camille Coduri and Romesh Ranganathan(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show

Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Crystal Palace at Etihad Stadium and Watford v Tottenham Hotspur at Vicarage Road. The Citizens were looking to avoid a repeat of their shock 3-2 defeat in last season's corresponding fixture, while Spurs faced a tough task against a Watford side that has been rejuvenated by Nigel Pearson. Plus, Arsenal v Sheffield United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Aston Villa, Norwich City v Bournemouth, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, West Ham United v Everton and Newcastle United v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Winter Diaries
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Snowdonia to discover how golden eagles - one of Britain's rarest birds of prey - are poised to return to the skies of Wales after an absence of 200 years(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores a property up for auction in Mountain Ash, south Wales, while residences in Gillingham, and Walsall are also examined(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Kinross on the banks of Loch Leven, visiting local children's hospice Rachel House to see its inspirational work. Plus, Eddie Reader sings Amazing Grace(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine presents an appeal on behalf of Parkinson's UK, which funds research, support networks and specialised nurses to help those with the condition(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a pair of memory filled gardens for widows Sue, who lives in Wallasey, Liverpool, and Anita, who resides in Fallowfield, Manchester(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, featuring a badly damaged suspended chair from the 1960s, a cast iron statue of Roman god Hercules, and a smokers' stand(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: It is 1903 and on the edge of Niagara Falls, something is wrong at Nikola Tesla's generator plant, where someone - or something - is sabotaging the maverick inventor's work. Has Tesla really received a message from Mars? And where does his great rival Thomas Edison fit into these events? The Doctor and her companions Yaz, Ryan and Graham must join forces with one of history's greatest minds to save both him and planet Earth. Guest starring Goran Visnjic, Robert Glenister and Anjli Mohindra(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward sister is off sick, but a lift malfunction sees her spend the evening with mother-to-be Connie instead of her planned rendezvous with Cyril. Nurse Crane tries to help a woman who is newly arrived from East Pakistan, while Shelagh enlists Violet, Trixie, Sister Frances and Valerie to put on a charity fashion show to raise money for the incubator fund(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Journalists from around the world descend on the Old Bailey as Stephen Ward's trial begins and the doctor trusts his friends in high places will come forward in support of his character. While Stephen faces the full force of the law and Mandy thrives on the publicity, Christine is terrified that her lie about Lucky Gordon will be revealed at the appeal. Starring Sophie Cookson and James Norton(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United at Anfield and Burnley v Leicester City at Turf Moor. Liverpool have been in dominant form this term, but United were the first team to take points off them this season when October's reverse fixture ended in a 1-1 draw, while Leicester will have been looking to capitalise on any slip-up from Jurgen Klopp's men with a victory over the Clarets(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Manchester City at SportNation.bet Stadium(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Holly Walker wants to start a new life in Australia, and hopes a trial week in sun-kissed Nelson, Victoria, will persuade husband Rob it is worth the move. Nicki Chapman presents(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last heat of the competition, the first three chefs recreate Monica's dish of oeufs meurette, before the next three take on Marcus' drop scones and a suzette sauce. In the next round, the six chefs are given a chance to showcase their creativity and cooking style as they present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the third quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers take a tour of Lincolnshire's pubs, and discover the important roles many of the county's establishments played in the Second World War, from a pub that was used as temporary accommodation for people made homeless by bombing to the Bluebell Inn in Woodhall Spa, which was the local for the pilots of the Dambuster squadron(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Newby Hall, North Yorkshire. Mark Stacey comes across two unusual tie and stick pins and Claire Rawle enthuses about a large collection of toy cars(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the county line from Devon into Somerset, seeing first-hand how willow farmers sought to overcome the challenge from the production of synthetic plastics during the 1930s and hearing how tourism has spread across the region, especially amongst the young. In Bath, Michael visits the former home of a refugee emperor, whose country was invaded by the Italian dictator Mussolini, and in whose name a religious movement began that now flourishes worldwide. Travelling east into Wiltshire, he reaches the largest training area of the British Army, Salisbury Plain(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the fourth quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London, played over the best of 11 frames. Neil Robertson was a 6-3 winner over Barry Hawkins at this stage last year, despite losing the first two frames of the match. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig finds herself stuck in a 'Quagmire', as she invites Sindhu Vee, Sally Phillips and Aisling Bea to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish posers. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Eshaan Akbar, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Sindhu Vee and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another. First shown in November(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her panel discuss the links between culture, money and power. In the wake of recent protests against multinational corporations funding some of the nation's major cultural institutions, they question whether landmark exhibitions, literary prizes and theatre productions should be backed by big business, as opposed to arguably more 'ethical' ways of funding the arts. The programme includes an exclusive interview with Bank of England Governor Mark Carney as he prepares to stand down from his post(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from the fourth quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents extended highlights of the third quarter-final on the sixth day at Alexandra Palace in London(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Having decided that everyone could do with a holiday, the Doctor takes Graham, Yasmin, Ryan to a luxury resort for a spot of rest and relaxation. However, they discover the place where they are having a break is hiding a number of deadly secrets. What are the ferocious monsters that are attacking Tranquillity Spa? Guest starring Laura Fraser and James Buckley, alongside Jodie Whittaker as the 13th incarnation of the Time Lord, and Bradley Walsh, Mandip Gill and Tosin Cole as her trusty travelling companions(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson plays clips featuring mice that invaded a farm in Australia and a swarm of locusts(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Chobham v St Oswald's(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team search for lethal beasts in Sri Lanka. In Yala National Park, they have an encounter with the elusive Sri Lankan leopard, then stake out a village to film an ancient river monster - the mugger crocodile. The presenter also witnesses the power of an enormous bone-crushing Indian rock python, before attempting to free dive with a blue whale, the largest animal on the planet(n)

programme.title: Supersized Earth
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines what it takes to keep seven billion humans alive, in terms of energy, food and water. Forty per cent of the planet's land surface is now devoted to growing food. To understand the sheer scale of this, the presenter paraglides over the world's largest collection of greenhouses on the south coast of Spain and joins the cowboys on Brazil's biggest cattle ranch as they herd more than 125,000 livestock. He also discovers how the man-made Lake Mead helped transform harsh desert into the bright lights of Las Vegas and joins the team building an artificial river from south to north China. Currently the world's most ambitious engineering project, it is an example of how the planet is being remodelled to man's needs(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the first of two festive specials in which four celebrity teams design their dream robot with the help of an experienced engineer, then battle it out in the arena - where all the same rules apply for the celebrities as they face off against each other and the imposing house robots. Who will become the Celebrity Robot Wars champions?(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: There are more new arrivals at the home, meaning Jimmy and Jenny Desmond now have 28 rescued chimpanzees to look after, including an eight-week-old baby called Gaia. Plans are coming together for the new sanctuary in the forest, and the orphans learn to master tools so they can forage for food. But a deadly cold breaks out among the babies, with disastrous consequences. The team also rescues an adult male chimp called Jonny who brings a fresh set of challenges. The pressure is on to move the chimps to their new home before things get out of hand(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Week three of the culinary sees Mary Berry ask the contestants to produce their ultimate Sunday brunch, after which Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish in just one hour using a sweet ingredient. They all push themselves to the limits to impress the judges, knowing that whoever serves up the least popular dishes have to compete in the eliminator round, for which chef Angela Hartnett has selected a technically complicated recipe(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to St Albans, Hertfordshire, to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin head to Japan, where they discover four extraordinary homes, built with great craftsmanship, combining high tech and a deep respect for tradition. Properties include the 'Glass House for a Diver' on the coast at Etajima, an all-glass building inspired by the chambers of an ants' nest, with rough concrete blocks chosen to surround the delicate home(n)

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny (1954)
programme.desc: US Navy officers rebel against their ship's erratic captain during a violent storm at sea, only to face a court martial back on dry land. However, the crew's actions seem to have been justified when evidence questioning the mental stability of their superior is produced. Second World War drama based on Herman Wouk's novel, starring Humphrey Bogart, Jose Ferrer, Fred MacMurray and Van Johnson(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents further coverage of the opening semi-final on the seventh day at Alexandra Palace in London, played over the best-of-11 frames. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and a team find out if eating less food can boost your memory, and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim investigates over-the-counter cannabis-based health products. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals how to spot a hidden illness that can cause stillbirth, and Alain Gregoire discovers the body's secret built-in stress reliever(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan explores equatorial regions in the Pacific, visiting some of the world's richest marine habitats. In the Galapagos Islands, Gordon discovers how drones are helping to protect wildlife including baby hammerhead sharks and iconic giant tortoises, while a mysterious tropical lake provides clues to the future of coral reefs(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the second semi-final on the seventh day at Alexandra Palace in London, played over the best-of-11 frames, as the line-up for the showpiece match in the prestigious non-ranking tournament is confirmed. The most successful player in the competition's history Ronnie O'Sullivan prevailed in the first of the semi-finals 12 months ago, overcoming Junhui 6-3, but the seven-time champion has decided not to enter this year's event, handing a place in the tournament to Ali Carter - ranked 17th in the world. The winner of the second semi-final in 2019 and the eventual winner of the title was Judd Trump, who defeated 2012 champion Neil Robertson 6-4. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Birdman (2014)
programme.desc: A faded movie star famous for his role in a series of superhero movies 20 years previously tries to reclaim his artistic integrity by directing and starring in a Broadway play. The experience plunges him into a personal crisis as both the production and his own family are beset by a series of disasters. Oscar-winning drama, starring Michael Keaton, Edward Norton and Emma Stone(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames on day seven at Alexandra Palace in London. The victorious player knew he would progress to Sunday's final, and have an opportunity to win the first Triple Crown title of the year(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Danny Clarke head to Croydon to create exotic, Jamaican-style garden for owner Carmen, who needs a tranquil space to remember her lost daughter(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don brings his globe-trotting botanical tour to a close with a visit to South East Asia, where he hopes to witness a true tropical oasis. Visiting the immaculately presented garden of Jim Thompson House in Bangkok, he discovers an exotic plot with a Western flavour. He also meets former president Lee Kuan Yew, who once stated that Singapore should be 'a city in a garden'(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Welney Wildlife Reserve in Norfolk, where Charlotte Smith helps out with a swan census and Steve Brown is on the trail of the elusive cranes(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Powderham Castle in Devon to spend time with the Earl and Countess of Devon and their two young children, who are reinventing the Earldom for the 21st century. Mary's time with the family reveals stories of romance and tragedy as she discovers more of this extraordinary home. In the castle's historic kitchen, Mary prepares a Devon cream tea, before making a delicate peach posset for a Midsummer Eve woodland party the family are throwing to celebrate their two year anniversary at the castle(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Daniel O'Donnell back to the islands of Arranmore and Owey off the coast of Co Donegal, where the singer reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1968(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening eight frames of the final from Alexandra Palace, London, where the best-of-19-frames match takes place. Judd Trump dominated last year's corresponding session, racing into a 7-1 lead against seven-time champion Ronnie O'Sullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire. Presenter Paul Martin visits nearby Renishaw Hall to learn about the Sitwell Trio - three siblings who took the artistic and literary world by storm in the early 20th century(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the World Cup men's downhill in Lauberhorn, and the Laax Open snowboarding contest, both which take place in Switzerland(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the concluding session of the final from Alexandra Palace, London, played over the best-of-19-frames. After going into the session facing a 7-1 deficit, Ronnie O'Sullivan fought back by winning two of the opening three frames, but Judd Trump was eventually able to prevail 10-4. It was the first time 'The Juddernaut' had won this title, and he went on to win the World Championship in the May of what was a memorable 2019 for him. The Bristolian also added the World Grand Prix title to his résumé, along with the International Championship, World Open and Northern Ireland Open later in the year. In November, he reached the final of the Champion of Champions event in Coventry, but lost 10-9 to Neil Robertson in a high-quality encounter. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Son of Saul (2015)
programme.desc: A Jewish prisoner in a Nazi concentration camp is forced to assist with the genocide being carried out. When he finds his own son's body, he vows to give the boy a proper burial instead of allowing his remains to be burned. As he looks for a rabbi to perform the ceremony, his fellow prisoners plot an uprising. Drama, starring Geza Rohrig and Levente Molnar. In Hungarian, Yiddish, German, Russian, Polish, French, Greek and Slovak(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Richard Skinner present the edition first shown on January 19, featuring performances by Roachford, Roy Orbison, Robert Howard and Kym Mazelle, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Ten City, Mica Paris and Will Downing, New Model Army, Brother Beyond, Natalie Cole, Mike and the Mechanics, Holly Johnson, Fine Young Cannibals, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, and Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and author reveals how the modern shape of the musical was established through a series of pioneering works, examining Jerome Kern and Oscar Hammerstein's Show Boat's bold look at America's racial divide in the 1920s. He also explores innovative uses of songs that further the narrative, referencing Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe's My Fair Lady, which made a star of Julie Andrews in the late 1950s(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Anthea Turner present the edition first shown on January 26, featuring performances by Then Jerico, Adeva, Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock, Mica Paris and Will Downing, Bobby Brown, Level 42, Sheena Easton, Ten City, Brother Beyond, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Milli Vanilli(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The musician explores the power music has to bring people together and to bond them in ways that are fundamental. His travels take him from the southern German cave where a 40,000-year-old bone flute was discovered to the modern-day mass singalong of New York's Choir! Choir! Choir! Along the way he gets to play with a Memphis marching band, join a song circle led by Bobby McFerrin, deconstruct the sexiness of Relax with its producer Trevor Horn, discuss the art of song-writing with his old Police colleague Sting and learn how to create dance floor unity with star DJ Honey Dijon(n)

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Musicians Midge Ure and Kim Appleby visit London and Manchester, the two cities that did battle with each other for musical pre-eminence as '80s music turned towards the new sounds of dance. This is a tale of how studio technology changed music - with British bands putting their own unique spin on dance to produce contrasting northern and southern sounds. Contributors include Denise Pearson from Five Star, Soul II Soul's Jazzie B, Mark Moore of S-Express, Shaun Ryder from the Happy Mondays and Peter Hook of New Order(n)

programme.title: I Can Go for That: The Smooth World of Yacht Rock
programme.desc: Katie Puckrik charts the progress of the genre through the 1980s, when it became the soundtrack to America in the Reagan era, and when artists like Toto, Hall & Oates and George Benson created a technicolour second wave of a super smooth sound. In the second of two programmes, yacht session supremos Jay Graydon and Steve Porcaro recall working with Benson. Porcaro joined other LA session players to form Toto and he and Steve Lukather reveal how they brought a little yacht magic to the biggest-selling album in history - Michael Jackson's Thriller. Other contributors include Robbie Dupree and John Oates(n)

programme.title: Stewart Copeland's Adventures in Music
programme.desc: The musician explores the power music has to bring people together and to bond them in ways that are fundamental. His travels take him from the southern German cave where a 40,000-year-old bone flute was discovered to the modern-day mass singalong of New York's Choir! Choir! Choir! Along the way he gets to play with a Memphis marching band, join a song circle led by Bobby McFerrin, deconstruct the sexiness of Relax with its producer Trevor Horn, discuss the art of song-writing with his old Police colleague Sting and learn how to create dance floor unity with star DJ Honey Dijon(n)

programme.title: Sound of Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and author reveals how the modern shape of the musical was established through a series of pioneering works, examining Jerome Kern and Oscar Hammerstein's Show Boat's bold look at America's racial divide in the 1920s. He also explores innovative uses of songs that further the narrative, referencing Alan Jay Lerner and Frederick Loewe's My Fair Lady, which made a star of Julie Andrews in the late 1950s(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The extraordinary abilities of the squirrel family are examined, putting their problem-solving to the test on a specially designed assault course. Some of the world's leading squirrel scientists reveal their groundbreaking discoveries - from the fox squirrel who can remember the location of 9,000 nuts, to the grey squirrel whose tree-top leaps are the basis of new designs in robotics(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present an insider guide to southern Wales, from the Severn Bridge to St Davids, as they unearth the coast's wild appeal. Tessa builds on 10 years of Coast stories from the area as she seeks new tales and extreme experiences for the guide, including coastal rowing, exploring why Gower was made Britain's first area of outstanding natural beauty, kayaking at Worm's head, and cockle picking at Penclawdd(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: William is suspended. leaving him unable to protect his reputation or officially investigate the supposedly wrongful conviction. Is there a killer still on the loose because of what he did 17 years ago? The rest of the team are determined to clear Wisting's name, but if he didn't tamper with the evidence, who did? In Norwegian and English(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: A young woman who wanted Benjamin to file a complaint about her stalker is reported missing, while Line makes an old connection from her past of which William does not approve(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Richard Skinner present the edition first shown on January 19, featuring performances by Roachford, Roy Orbison, Robert Howard and Kym Mazelle, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, Ten City, Mica Paris and Will Downing, New Model Army, Brother Beyond, Natalie Cole, Mike and the Mechanics, Holly Johnson, Fine Young Cannibals, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, and Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Anthea Turner present the edition first shown on January 26, featuring performances by Then Jerico, Adeva, Rob Base and DJ E-Z Rock, Mica Paris and Will Downing, Bobby Brown, Level 42, Sheena Easton, Ten City, Brother Beyond, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Milli Vanilli(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from(n)

programme.title: Sings Musicals
programme.desc: Memorable versions of songs taken from or inspired by musicals. The programme features Ella Fitzgerald singing Mack the Knife, from the Threepenny Opera, Captain Sensible's 1982 chart-topper Happy Talk, a cover of a song from South Pacific, and performances by Jeff Beck and Jay-Z(n)

programme.title: Ice Dream: Lapland's Snow Show
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton reports from Lapland, where some of the biggest names in art and architecture - including Zaha Hadid, Anish Kapoor and Future Systems - gathered in 2004 to produce an extraordinary exhibition of works made from ice and snow(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present an insider guide to southern Wales, from the Severn Bridge to St Davids, as they unearth the coast's wild appeal. Tessa builds on 10 years of Coast stories from the area as she seeks new tales and extreme experiences for the guide, including coastal rowing, exploring why Gower was made Britain's first area of outstanding natural beauty, kayaking at Worm's head, and cockle picking at Penclawdd(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Wales and discover a strong industrial engineering history, sailing across Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, the highest navigable aqueduct in the world. The tiny village of Hawarden gives the duo the opportunity to stay overnight in the UK's only residential library, before they make an attempt on the summit of Mount Snowdon itself - via the mountain's iconic rack-and-pinion narrow gauge steam railway train(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Manchester City at SportNation.bet Stadium(n)

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: The presenter tells the story of the 17th-century residence in Derbyshire - the pleasure palace of playboy Cavalier and ambitious courtier William Cavendish. Through a collection of artworks and items held within the castle, Lucy uncovers a colourful tale, capturing the tensions of England in the 1600s that would eventually lead the nation to civil war(n)

programme.title: All Aboard! New Zealand by Rail, Sea and Land
programme.desc: Cameras take an epic overland journey, by train, boat and car through New Zealand's breathtaking landscapes. The trek beings in Auckland, but the city soon gives way to rolling pastures, volcanic extremes, tranquil waterways, the snowcapped grandeur of the Southern Alps and the beauty of Fiordland(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure(n)

Unfortunately, Grandmaster Glitch has built a snow princess at the top of the fall and is using an ice machine to stop her melting. programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but Charlie Ross manages to rip his trousers. At the auction at Battle, East Sussex, Izzie's silver brooch goes against Charlie's silver pen(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard takes to the dance floor to make a vegan meal that will get a troupe of ballroom dancers foxtrotting to a straight 10. With a sprinkle of flare, a dollop of panache and a pinch of style, Matt aims to show the waltzing wonders how plant-based food can stand up to any conventional British classic dish(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush is thrilled to learn Sonia and Martin are back together but when the latter arrives home he finds a letter that leaves him rocked. Honey forces herself to eat in front of the children, making herself feel unwell, while Dotty continues to try to charm Leo - only for him to show his true colours when she mentions his father. Jean and Daniel are the happiest they have ever been, and Rainie sets up a date for Callum(n)

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics group the Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society undertakes a 1960s-set horror tale, with Robert as the mysterious Albert Fortenoy welcoming a young family to his crumbling old house. A number of clever theatrical horror devices are used to create a creepy atmosphere and plenty of jump scares - and every single one manages to go horribly wrong. Worse, the designers have taken 'crumbling' to heart and the cast members proceed to fall off, out of and through everything. Comedy, starring Henry Lewis, Jonathan Sayer and Henry Shields(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Steph McGovern, Richard Osman, Jennifer Saunders and Joe Sugg join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies(n)

programme.title: The Tuckers
programme.desc: Billy strikes a deal with Clock to buy some dodgy wine. Meanwhile, Glyn gets in trouble with a local gangster and his violent sons. Comedy, written by and starring Steve Speirs, with Robert Pugh, Alexandria Riley, Joshua McCord, Ben McGregor and Jams Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Michael B Jordan discuss starring in legal drama Just Mercy, while actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders talks about new mystery thriller series The Stranger, in which she features. Plus, Patrick Stewart reflects on his decision to reprise his most famous role in latest sci-fi spin-off Star Trek: Picard. Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero and more intrepid audience members attempt to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: King Gary

Gary throws absolutely everything at getting him back and even turns up, unannounced, at Abboussi's father's workplace to try to convince him that his son is making a big mistake. programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max attempt to find a masked assailant who stole the famous Icon of St Nicholas, but the escape of a person of interest results in a furious Ines removing the pair from the case, causing a rift between the two detectives. When the icon suddenly reappears, Miranda and Max must put their differences aside as they are drawn into a furious cat-and-mouse chase(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the third week of auditions, and the audience still hold the power, as only they will decide if the mirror opens for the acts or not. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition, while one Dance Captain will get to pick an act and take them straight through to the live shows. programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring comedians Sofie Hagen, Jenny Eclair, Cariad Lloyd, Darren Harriott, Nish Kumar, Ed Gamble, Simon Evans and Geoff Norcott(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith brings her son Luka to the ED after he falls from his sister's bunk bed, injuring his head and wrist. Dylan treats the youngster, and becomes more concerned that there is an underlying issue, and following a CT scan, he is diagnosed with a brain tumour. A concerned Charlie has to placate a distressed Duffy, who has been trying to phone Bill in the early hours of the morning(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Crystal Palace at Etihad Stadium and Watford v Tottenham Hotspur at Vicarage Road. The Citizens were looking to avoid a repeat of their shock 3-2 defeat in last season's corresponding fixture, while Spurs faced a tough task against a Watford side that has been rejuvenated by Nigel Pearson. Plus, Arsenal v Sheffield United, Brighton & Hove Albion v Aston Villa, Norwich City v Bournemouth, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers, West Ham United v Everton and Newcastle United v Chelsea(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman returns with former NFL stars Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to preview the two Conference Championship matches taking place this weekend(n)

programme.title: The Spectacular Now (2013)
programme.desc: A teenage loner wakes up one morning to find a classmate passed out drunk on her lawn. They form an unlikely friendship, and he offers her help in constructing a new persona for herself that will boost her confidence - but begins to fall in love with the act she is putting on. Drama based on the novel by Tim Tharp, starring Miles Teller, Shailene Woodley and Brie Larson(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams(n)

programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Snowdonia to discover how golden eagles - one of Britain's rarest birds of prey - are poised to return to the skies of Wales after an absence of 200 years(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi-detached house in Folkestone in Kent, and a terraced property in Hanley, Stoke-on-Trent, are sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In south Wales, Kate and Teg set a mountain alight and experience the heat of a controlled burn with the local fire service in an area with the highest incidence of wild fires in the whole of the UK. Kate gets the creative juices flowing by lending a hand to the creation of an epic land art piece - a 30ft mandala - a stunning geometrically patterned circle made entirely from natural materials. Teg gets a run for her money, when meeting film actors at a rural Film Academy for Animals, and Kate fulfils her childhood dream with a master farrier, learning how to shoe a horse(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Gary Pe help two teams hunt for items at the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, while Eric Knowles explores the Japanese concept of wabi-sabi in pottery(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Kinross on the banks of Loch Leven, visiting local children's hospice Rachel House to see its inspirational work. Plus, Eddie Reader sings Amazing Grace(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine presents an appeal on behalf of Parkinson's UK, which funds research, support networks and specialised nurses to help those with the condition(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an American expat couple with a generous £1million budget to search for a home in Surrey. Away from the house hunt, the presenter finds out about the county's motor racing history, and goes out on a track in a 1929 Bentley(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a pair of memory filled gardens for widows Sue, who lives in Wallasey, Liverpool, and Anita, who resides in Fallowfield, Manchester(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, featuring a badly damaged suspended chair from the 1960s, a cast iron statue of Roman god Hercules, and a smokers' stand(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Adam Gemili, tennis star Heather Watson, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott and ex-West Ham United striker Dean Ashton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The 1900 Island
programme.desc: The seven-strong Davies family are running out of supplies so Lydia instigates a food hamper to help, but not everyone shares her charitable vision. As the weather picks up, the men head out to sea for a three-day fishing trip, leaving their wives and children anxiously awaiting their return(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: It is 1903 and on the edge of Niagara Falls, something is wrong at Nikola Tesla's generator plant, where someone - or something - is sabotaging the maverick inventor's work. Has Tesla really received a message from Mars? And where does his great rival Thomas Edison fit into these events? The Doctor and her companions Yaz, Ryan and Graham must join forces with one of history's greatest minds to save both him and planet Earth. Guest starring Goran Visnjic, Robert Glenister and Anjli Mohindra(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward sister is off sick, but a lift malfunction sees her spend the evening with mother-to-be Connie instead of her planned rendezvous with Cyril. Nurse Crane tries to help a woman who is newly arrived from East Pakistan, while Shelagh enlists Violet, Trixie, Sister Frances and Valerie to put on a charity fashion show to raise money for the incubator fund(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Journalists from around the world descend on the Old Bailey as Stephen Ward's trial begins and the doctor trusts his friends in high places will come forward in support of his character. While Stephen faces the full force of the law and Mandy thrives on the publicity, Christine is terrified that her lie about Lucky Gordon will be revealed at the appeal. Starring Sophie Cookson and James Norton(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Liverpool v Manchester United at Anfield and Burnley v Leicester City at Turf Moor. Liverpool have been in dominant form this term, but United were the first team to take points off them this season when October's reverse fixture ended in a 1-1 draw, while Leicester will have been looking to capitalise on any slip-up from Jurgen Klopp's men with a victory over the Clarets(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Meadow Park and Birmingham City v Manchester City at SportNation.bet Stadium(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits an old workshop in Margate in Kent and a building plot in Newcastle-under-Lyme. Martel Maxwell is at a semi-detached house in Wednesbury, West Midlands, that has some worrying cracks(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited

programme.title: Caught Red Handed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi gets drawn into the murky side of the prison. Emma has to take a stand when there is a patient revolt(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson finds himself back in Somerset with a couple whose rural aspirations needed to accommodate their own needs, plus those of their parents.
